body.title: Prompt-first LLM memory type classifier with hash cache
body.result: llm_classifier.py: classify() returns (memory_type, cloud_calls_count); SHA256 cache in competition.classifications_cache; 7 tests pass
body.intention: I intend that the prompt-first classifier establishes an LLM quality baseline with tracked cloud cost per classification.
body.description:
Build Personality B: classify input text via structured LLM prompt → memory_type. Cache results by SHA256(text) in a local file or competition schema table to avoid repeat cloud calls. Second call for same text = 0 cloud calls.

**Affected files:** New `lab/competition/classifiers/llm_classifier.py`; uses existing LLM client
**Design rules:** Cache is mandatory — without it the classifier burns cloud calls on every eval row on every run
**Scope boundary:** classify(text) → memory_type + cloud_calls_count. Few-shot prompt with 3-5 examples per type.
**Completion criteria:** `llm_classifier.classify(text)` returns (memory_type, cloud_calls_count); second call for identical text returns cloud_calls_count=0; 5 sample programming-book sentences produce plausible types.
